obligations.reason: The scope exposes JTAG pins, SimDTM/SimJTAG/dmi_jtag instances, debug_req_o, ndmreset_o, dmactive_o, jtag_hash and jtag_unlock signals. Visible RTL should prove that debug/test subjects cannot activate protected debug capabilities without authorization.

evidence_slots.subject: external JTAG/test/debug requester via SimDTM/SimJTAG/dmi_jtag or tck_i/tms_i/td_i
evidence_slots.operation: issue DMI/debug request and potentially trigger debug_req_o/ndmreset_o/dmactive_o or debug bus access
evidence_slots.object: debug_req/debug_resp DMI path, debug_req_o/ndmreset_o/dmactive_o and dm_master/dm_slave debug bus signals
evidence_slots.expected_guard: debug authorization/unlock derived from jtag_hash/jtag_unlock or equivalent must mediate DMI/debug requests before protected debug/reset/bus effects
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: riscv_peripherals declares jtag_hash and jtag_unlock; dmi_jtag is connected to jtag_hash_i/okey_hash_i/ikey_hash_i and jtag_unlock_o, but under RISCV_FESVR_SIM the SimDTM instance drives debug_req_valid/debug_req directly and no jtag_unlock guard is present in the observed SimDTM path. Debug NoC bridge also connects to dm_master signals, but complete downstream authorization use was not observed.
evidence_slots.path: RISCV_FESVR_SIM: SimDTM -> debug_req_valid/debug_req -> debug module path; non-sim: tck_i/tms_i/td_i -> dmi_jtag(jtag_hash_i, jtag_unlock_o) -> debug_req/debug_req_valid
evidence_slots.state_condition: RISCV_FESVR_SIM or RISCV_JTAG_SIM build configuration; physical JTAG path uses dmi_jtag but reset/default of jtag_unlock and downstream gating not fully observed
evidence_slots.impact: An unauthenticated simulation/test debug requester may obtain privileged debug control, halt/reset harts, or perform debug bus transactions that bypass normal software privilege and access-control policy.

rtl_evidence.detail: jtag_hash/ikey_hash/okey_hash and jtag_unlock signals declared.
SimDTM directly connects debug_req_valid, debug_req.addr/op/data, debug_resp without visible jtag_unlock guard.
dmi_jtag receives jtag_hash_i/okey_hash_i/ikey_hash_i and outputs jtag_unlock_o while driving DMI request/response.
debug NoC AXI-lite bridge connects to dm_master signals.

inspection_records.missing_or_uncertain_evidence: Complete debug module instantiation and downstream use of jtag_unlock for debug_req_o/ndmreset_o/dmactive_o were not fully present in provided observations.
inspection_records.candidate_finding: Simulation debug paths can issue DMI/debug requests without the jtag_hash/jtag_unlock authorization path, while physical dmi_jtag receives jtag_hash/okey_hash/ikey_hash and emits jtag_unlock. The visible FESVR SimDTM path drives debug_req/debug_req_valid directly with no observed unlock guard.
inspection_records.notes: Finding is strongest for simulation/test configurations; physical JTAG may be mediated inside dmi_jtag, but that module body was not provided.

evidence_slots.subject: software/MMIO or NoC requester reaching pkt_wrapper through buf_ariane_pkt_noc2_*
evidence_slots.operation: write fuse request/address and read fuse data or public-key-location values
evidence_slots.object: fuse_addr_o/fuse_req_o, fuse_rdata_i, pkey_loc_o, fuse_mem contents including JTAG/HMAC/AES/SHA/access-control data and jtag_hash_o
evidence_slots.expected_guard: fuse read/address selection should be mediated by acct_ctrl_i plus register-lock bits and bounds checks before arbitrary fuse addresses or pkey locations can be read
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: pkt_wrapper gates wrapper enable with acct_ctrl_i, but write cases for fuse_req_o and fuse_addr_o do not check reglk_ctrl_i. Read cases return pkey_loc high/low only if reglk_ctrl_i[4]/[5] are clear and fuse_rdata_i if reglk_ctrl_i[6] is clear. pkt instance has req_i tied to 1 and consumes fuse_addr_o as fuse_indx_i. Fuse memory contains security-sensitive constants and has direct req_i/addr_i/rdata_o and jtag_hash_o ports.
evidence_slots.path: NoC pkt port -> noc_axilite_bridge -> pkt_wrapper en=en_acct&&acct_ctrl_i -> fuse_req_o/fuse_addr_o -> fuse_mem req_i/addr_i/rdata_o and pkt pkey_loc_o read mux
evidence_slots.state_condition: If acct_ctrl_i permits the current priv_lvl and reglk_ctrl_i[6] is not set, fuse_rdata_i is returned; regardless of lock bits, writes to fuse_req_o/fuse_addr_o are accepted when en&&we. No observed bounds check on fuse_addr_o before driving fuse_mem.
evidence_slots.impact: A permitted or mis-permitted MMIO requester can select arbitrary fuse indices and read or locate fuse-backed keys/hashes/access-control constants, enabling disclosure of crypto material and debug-authentication/reference data.

rtl_evidence.detail: en = en_acct && acct_ctrl_i; fuse_req_o and fuse_addr_o written on en&&we with no reglk_ctrl_i check.
read mux returns fuse_rdata_i at case 4 when reglk_ctrl_i[6] is clear; pkey_loc halves gated by bits 4 and 5; default only zeros for fuse_addr_o <= 110.
pkt instance has req_i tied to 1'b1 and fuse_indx_i=fuse_addr_o.
fuse memory constant array includes JTAG expected hash, RNG polynomials, HMAC/AES/SHA keys, and access-control data.
pkt_wrapper wired to reglk_ctrl[5] and acc_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i][5], and to fuse_req/fuse_addr/fuse_rdata.

inspection_records.missing_or_uncertain_evidence: The remaining fuse_mem read logic after line 127 is truncated, so exact req_i/addr_i behavior and jtag_hash_o assignment are incomplete in observations.
inspection_records.candidate_finding: pkt_wrapper allows an authorized-by-acct AXI requester to program fuse_req_o and arbitrary fuse_addr_o without reglk protection on the address/control writes; reads can expose fuse_rdata_i when reglk_ctrl_i[6] is clear, and pkt always computes pkey_loc_o from fuse_addr_o with req_i tied high.
inspection_records.notes: This is a candidate because access depends on acct_ctrl_i configuration, but address/control writes lack local lock enforcement and fuse read exposure is explicit when unlocked.

evidence_slots.subject: software/MMIO or debug-mode requester through RNG NoC/AXI wrapper
evidence_slots.operation: write RNG polynomial controls or read seed/internal segments/control state
evidence_slots.object: poly128/poly64/poly32/poly16 controls, seed128_o/seed64_o/seed32_o/seed16_o, rand_seg*_o, cs_state_o, rand_num_o
evidence_slots.expected_guard: acct_ctrl_i/reglk_ctrl_i should restrict writes and reads, and debug visibility should require explicit debug authorization rather than debug_mode_i alone
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: wrapper enable is acct_ctrl_i-gated, but write logic uses debug_mode_i ? wdata : (reglk_ctrl_i[...] ? old : wdata), so debug_mode_i bypasses lock bits for polynomial/control writes. Read logic for addresses 15-25 returns seed outputs, entropy segment outputs, and cs_state_o directly when debug_mode_i is high, otherwise masks based on reglk_ctrl_i[7]. No jtag_unlock/debug auth input is present on rng_wrapper.
evidence_slots.path: NoC RNG port -> axi_lite_interface -> rng_wrapper en=en_acct&&acct_ctrl_i -> register write/read mux -> rng_top seed/poly/load -> seed*_o/rand_seg*_o/cs_state_o -> AXI response
evidence_slots.state_condition: debug_mode_i asserted; acct_ctrl_i permits access. Reset rst_10 resets load/load_cnt but seed/poly register reset coverage is incomplete; rng_cs reset behavior appears inverted in observed code.
evidence_slots.impact: A requester that can enter or spoof debug_mode_i can reconfigure RNG behavior and read internal seeds/segments/state, making RNG outputs predictable or leaking entropy state to software/debug paths.

rtl_evidence.detail: rng_wrapper has reglk_ctrl_i, acct_ctrl_i, debug_mode_i, AXI ports, and rst_10.
en is acct-gated; polynomial write example uses debug_mode_i to bypass reglk_ctrl_i[5].
debug_mode_i causes seed128/64/32/16, rand_seg, and cs_state reads to return directly, bypassing reglk_ctrl_i[7] masking.
rng_top connected to seed/poly inputs and debug/internal outputs seed*_o, rand_seg*_o, cs_state_o.
rng_top exposes seed segment outputs, rand segment outputs, and cs_state_o.
rng_cs exposes rand_num, rand segment outputs, and cs_state_o derived from entropy sources.

inspection_records.missing_or_uncertain_evidence: Full write-case coverage for all polynomial registers was truncated, and the source/authorization semantics of debug_mode_i were not provided.
inspection_records.candidate_finding: rng_wrapper lets debug_mode_i bypass reglk_ctrl_i protections for polynomial writes and for reads of seed/internal RNG state, without any observed debug authorization such as jtag_unlock.
inspection_records.notes: The explicit debug_mode_i bypass of lock masking supports a permission candidate even though acct_ctrl_i still gates en.

evidence_slots.subject: software/MMIO or debug-mode requester through RSA NoC/AXI wrapper
evidence_slots.operation: configure RSA operation, write operands/message, read result/status
evidence_slots.object: RSA inter_rst/inter_rst1/encrypt_decrypt controls, p_i/q_i/msg_in operands, msg_out/exe_finish status
evidence_slots.expected_guard: writes to RSA key/modulus/message/control registers and reads of result should be mediated by acct_ctrl_i/reglk_ctrl_i; debug_mode_i must not bypass controls without authorization
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: rsa_wrapper gates local enable with acct_ctrl_i. Observed write cases update control, prime, and msg_in registers only under en&&we and preserve old values when reglk_ctrl_i[3] is set. Observed read cases for msg_out/status return zero when reglk_ctrl_i[3] is set. debug_mode_i is a port but no observed read/write condition uses it to bypass reglk_ctrl_i.
evidence_slots.path: NoC RSA port -> axi_lite_interface -> rsa_wrapper en=en_acct&&acct_ctrl_i -> reglk_ctrl_i[3]-guarded control/operand writes and msg_out/status read mux -> rsa_top
evidence_slots.state_condition: rst_13 or rst_ni reset clears inter_rst, inter_rst1, encry_decry_i, prime_i, prime1_i, and msg_in; msg_out is produced by rsa_top and not reset in wrapper. Access allowed only when acct_ctrl_i is high and reglk_ctrl_i[3] is clear for sensitive read/write.
evidence_slots.impact: If the observed guards hold for all cases, unprivileged/debug-mode requesters cannot modify RSA operands/control or read results when acct/lock policy denies access; residual msg_out reset behavior remains a separate uncertainty.

rtl_evidence.detail: en = en_acct && acct_ctrl_i; exe_finish_o forced complete on rst_13.
reset clears controls/operands; write cases use reglk_ctrl_i[3] ? old : wdata for control and prime fields.
msg_in write cases continue to be gated by reglk_ctrl_i[3].
msg_out/status read cases return zero when reglk_ctrl_i[3] is set.
rsa_top instance receives guarded inputs and produces msg_out/exe_finish.
rsa_top consumes p_i/q_i/msg_in/encrypt_decrypt_i and outputs msg_out/mod_exp_finish_o.

inspection_records.missing_or_uncertain_evidence: Full middle section of rsa_wrapper read/write cases is truncated; reset behavior of msg_out inside rsa_top/mod_exp is not visible; policy register correctness is covered separately.
inspection_records.notes: No candidate debug_mode_i bypass was observed for RSA; status marked satisfied for local wrapper guard use with reset residue uncertainty noted.

evidence_slots.subject: software/MMIO requester or debug/test path reaching acct_wrapper/reglk_wrapper
evidence_slots.operation: write/clear/configure acc_ctrl and reglk_ctrl policy bits
evidence_slots.object: reglk_ctrl and acc_ctrl policy state fanout to peripheral wrappers
evidence_slots.expected_guard: policy registers must be privileged/authorized, safe on reset, sticky once locked, and not clearable through alternate/debug/test paths
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: riscv_peripherals declares reglk_ctrl and acc_ctrl, derives acc_ctrl_c by slicing acc_ctrl with an OR alias for peripheral 5, and fans slices to wrappers. acct_wrapper drives acc_ctrl_o and receives reglk_ctrl_i/acct_ctrl_i/we_flag; reglk_wrapper drives reglk_ctrl_o. However, internal acct_wrapper/reglk_wrapper write, reset, and sticky semantics were not provided.
evidence_slots.path: NoC acct/reglk ports -> noc_axilite_bridge -> acct_wrapper/reglk_wrapper -> acc_ctrl/reglk_ctrl -> acc_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i][peripheral] and wrapper reglk slices
evidence_slots.state_condition: Reset defaults, lock-to-unlock transitions, and we_flag effects are unknown. reglk_ctrl slice for one wrapper includes an OR with we_flag_1; acct_wrapper receives we_flag_0.
evidence_slots.impact: If policy registers are writable by unprivileged requesters, reset insecurely, or non-sticky, downstream per-peripheral guards can be disabled, enabling broad access to crypto, fuse, reset, and debug-related peripherals.

rtl_evidence.detail: reglk_ctrl and acc_ctrl declared; acc_ctrl_c generated from acc_ctrl and special OR for j==5 with peripheral 4 bits.
rst_wrapper receives reglk_ctrl slice, acc_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i][12], debug_mode_i.
pkt_wrapper receives reglk_ctrl[5] and acc_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i][5].
acct_wrapper drives acc_ctrl_o and receives reglk/acct slices plus we_flag_0.
reglk_wrapper drives reglk_ctrl_o and receives reglk/acct slices.

inspection_records.missing_or_uncertain_evidence: acct_wrapper and reglk_wrapper RTL bodies, reset values, write-enable conditions, and sticky lock semantics are absent from observations.
inspection_records.notes: Cannot prove a candidate violation or satisfaction without policy register internals.

evidence_slots.subject: software/MMIO requester via AXI/NoC CLINT path
evidence_slots.operation: read/write timer/IPI MMIO state through en_o/we_o
evidence_slots.object: downstream CLINT timer and inter-processor interrupt state
evidence_slots.expected_guard: CLINT timer/IPI writes should be constrained by privilege/access policy or address-range mediation, with errors on unauthorized/out-of-range access
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: axi_lite_interface records the transaction address and generates en_o/we_o for accepted reads/writes; response b.resp/r.resp are tied to 2'b0 and no address range or privilege signal is present in the interface. Provided top-level PLIC adapter similarly subtracts base and forwards requests without privilege checks, but downstream CLINT register implementation was not shown.
evidence_slots.path: AXI requester -> axi_lite_interface -> address_o/en_o/we_o/data_o -> downstream timer/IPI registers
evidence_slots.state_condition: On reset, state/address/id registers reset per state machine; out-of-range/unauthorized behavior has no observed error path because responses are tied OKAY.
evidence_slots.impact: A requester that reaches the CLINT AXI-lite port may modify timer/IPI state or probe unintended addresses without hardware privilege rejection, potentially causing unauthorized interrupts, timer manipulation, or denial of service.

rtl_evidence.detail: Interface exposes AXI request/response and generic address_o/en_o/we_o/data ports; no privilege input.
read/write response data/id assigned; b.resp and r.resp tied to 2'b0 OKAY.
state machine accepts aw_valid/ar_valid and captures address without address-range checks in observed snippet.
top-level exposes CLINT NoC ports.

inspection_records.missing_or_uncertain_evidence: Actual downstream CLINT register module and any top-level address filtering outside axi_lite_interface were not provided.
inspection_records.candidate_finding: The shared axi_lite_interface used for CLINT-style MMIO performs no privilege, address-range, or error-response enforcement; it accepts AXI read/write handshakes and emits en_o/we_o/address_o while always returning OKAY responses.
inspection_records.notes: Candidate is limited to visible interface behavior; a downstream CLINT module could still implement range/permission checks, but no evidence was provided.

evidence_slots.subject: NoC DMA/debug/reset/peripheral requester
evidence_slots.operation: route read/write transactions to protected peripheral targets and reset/control endpoints
evidence_slots.object: crypto/fuse/reglk/rst/peripheral register spaces and reset/control signals
evidence_slots.expected_guard: fabric routing must preserve per-target permissions and requester attributes; DMA/debug/reset endpoints should not reach protected controls without target-specific access checks
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: riscv_peripherals exposes many separate NoC ports. For pkt and reset bridges, AXI aw/ar prot/cache/qos/region and ids are tied to zero constants. Protected wrappers receive acct_ctrl_i computed from acc_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i][target], where priv_lvl_i is a top-level core privilege input, not a per-NoC packet attribute. Debug NoC bridge connects directly to dm_master AXI signals. No observed access-control matrix uses NoC source identity.
evidence_slots.path: NoC endpoint ports -> noc_axilite_bridge -> per-target AXI request -> wrapper en_acct/acct_ctrl_i or debug dm_master -> protected peripheral/register state
evidence_slots.state_condition: When global priv_lvl_i and acc_ctrl permit a target, any requester on the corresponding NoC endpoint may be treated equivalently; default/tied AXI attributes do not convey DMA/debug/source privilege.
evidence_slots.impact: A malicious or compromised fabric endpoint may be authorized according to the core's current privilege rather than its own identity, enabling unauthorized access to crypto, fuse, reset, or debug-related register spaces.

rtl_evidence.detail: top-level exposes separate NoC ports for RSA, RNG, debug, CLINT, AES, reset, SHA, HMAC, PKT, ACCT, REGLK, DMA, etc.
acc_ctrl_c derived only from acc_ctrl and index; later wrappers select acc_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i][peripheral].
reset wrapper guarded by acc_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i][12] and connected to rst_axi_req/resp.
pkt wrapper guarded by acc_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i][5] and connected to pkt_axi_req/resp.
pkt AXI attributes id/len/size/burst/lock/cache/prot/qos/region tied to constants including prot=0.
reset AXI attributes similarly tied to constants including prot=0.
debug NoC bridge connects to dm_master address/data handshake signals.

inspection_records.missing_or_uncertain_evidence: NoC packet format, splitter address map, and noc_axilite_bridge internals were not provided, so source reachability and endpoint isolation are partially inferred from top-level connections.
inspection_records.candidate_finding: NoC-to-AXI-lite bridges route independent NoC endpoints to protected wrappers while AXI privilege/protection attributes are tied off and wrapper access decisions use global priv_lvl_i-derived acc_ctrl rather than requester-carried attributes. This can lose source identity for DMA/debug/reset/fabric requesters.
inspection_records.notes: Candidate focuses on attribute/source-identity loss at visible routing boundaries.

evidence_slots.subject: software/external reset requester through reset NoC endpoint or we_flag/control path
evidence_slots.operation: assert rst_5/rst_10/rst_13 and other peripheral resets
evidence_slots.object: pkt/rng/rsa reset controls and residual fuse/RNG/RSA state
evidence_slots.expected_guard: reset triggers for sensitive peripherals must be privilege/access controlled, must not bypass locks, and should completely clear protected state and stale outputs
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: riscv_peripherals instantiates rst_wrapper on a NoC AXI-lite bridge and outputs rst_1..rst_13 to wrappers. pkt_wrapper resets fuse_req_o/fuse_addr_o on rst_5; rng_wrapper resets load/load_cnt on rst_10 but observed reset block for seed/poly writes is empty; rsa_wrapper resets controls/operands/msg_in on rst_13 but leaves msg_out clearing to rsa_top/mod_exp and forces exe_finish_o to 1 when rst_13 is asserted. The reset NoC bridge ties AXI protection attributes to constants.
evidence_slots.path: buf_ariane_rst_noc2_* -> noc_axilite_bridge -> rst_wrapper -> rst_5/rst_10/rst_13 -> pkt/rng/rsa wrapper reset logic and protected outputs
evidence_slots.state_condition: When rst_5/rst_10/rst_13 asserted; if reset policy allows requester via acc_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i][12]. Lock clearing/preservation inside rst_wrapper and policy wrappers was not observed.
evidence_slots.impact: An unauthorized or over-permitted reset requester may disrupt crypto/RNG/fuse-related peripherals, bypass in-progress operations or lock assumptions, and potentially leave stale RNG/RSA outputs readable after partial reset.

rtl_evidence.detail: rst_1..rst_13 reset signals declared.
rst_wrapper receives reglk_ctrl slice, acc_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i][12], debug_mode_i and outputs rst_1..rst_13.
reset NoC endpoint bridged to rst_axi_req/resp.
on ~(rst_ni && ~rst_5), fuse_req_o and fuse_addr_o reset to 0; otherwise writes accepted.
rst_10 resets load/load_cnt; write-side reset branch is empty for polynomial/seed-related registers in observed snippet.
rst_13 forces exe_finish_o and reset branch clears controls/operands/msg_in but msg_out clear is commented/not in wrapper.
rsa_top reset inputs gated with ~rst_13.

inspection_records.missing_or_uncertain_evidence: rst_wrapper RTL, reset policy register semantics, full RNG reset logic for all registers, and mod_exp/msg_out reset behavior were not provided.
inspection_records.candidate_finding: Security-sensitive peripheral resets are software/NoC reachable through rst_wrapper and can clear wrapper state; reset control is guarded only by acc_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i][12]/reglk slice, with no observed per-source authorization or proof that resets preserve locks and fully clear outputs.
inspection_records.notes: Candidate combines reset reachability with incomplete clearing/authorization evidence; severity depends on rst_wrapper policy configuration.

86:                 default:
87:                     ;
88:             endcase
89:     end // always @ (posedge wb_clk_i)
90: 
91: //// Read side

100:             1:

102:             2:
103:                 rdata = reglk_ctrl_i[4] ? 'b0 : pkey_loc[63:32];
104:             3:
105:                 rdata = reglk_ctrl_i[5] ? 'b0 : pkey_loc[31:0];
106:             4:
107:                 rdata = reglk_ctrl_i[6] ? 'b0 : fuse_rdata_i;
108:             default:
109:                 if (fuse_addr_o <= 110)
110:                     rdata = 32'b0;
111:         endcase
112:       end // if
113:     end // always @ (*)
114: 
115: pkt # (

121:             .fuse_indx_i(fuse_addr_o),
122:             .pkey_loc_o(pkey_loc)
123:         );
124:

16: module axi_lite_interface #(
17:     parameter int unsigned AXI_ADDR_WIDTH = 64,
18:     parameter int unsigned AXI_DATA_WIDTH = 64,
19:     parameter int unsigned AXI_ID_WIDTH   = 10
20: ) (
21:     input logic                       clk_i,    // Clock
22:     input logic                       rst_ni,  // Asynchronous reset active low
23: 
24:     input  ariane_axi::req_t          axi_req_i,
25:     output ariane_axi::resp_t         axi_resp_o,
26: 
27:     output logic [AXI_ADDR_WIDTH-1:0] address_o,
28:     output logic                      en_o,        // transaction is valid
29:     output logic                      we_o,        // write
30:     input  logic [AXI_DATA_WIDTH-1:0] data_i,      // data
31:     output logic [AXI_DATA_WIDTH-1:0] data_o
32: );
33: 
34:     // The RLAST signal is not required, and is considered asserted for every transfer on the read data channel.
35:     enum logic [1:0] { IDLE, READ, WRITE, WRITE_B } state_q, state_d;
36:     // save the trans id, we will need it for reflection otherwise we are not plug compatible to the AXI standard
37:     logic [AXI_ID_WIDTH-1:0]   trans_id_n, trans_id_q;
38:     // address register
39:     logic [AXI_ADDR_WIDTH-1:0] address_n,  address_q;
40: 
41:     // pass through read data on the read data channel
42:     assign axi_resp_o.r.data = data_i;
43:     // send back the transaction id we've latched
44:     assign axi_resp_o.r.id = trans_id_q;
45:     assign axi_resp_o.b.id = trans_id_q;
46:     // set r_last to one as defined by the AXI4 - Lite standard
47:     assign axi_resp_o.r.last = 1'b1;
48:     // we do not support any errors so set response flag to all zeros
49:     assign axi_resp_o.b.resp = 2'b0;
50:     assign axi_resp_o.r.resp = 2'b0;
51:     // output data which we want to write to the slave
52:     assign data_o = axi_req_i.w.data;
53:     // ------------------------
54:     // AXI4-Lite State Machine
55:     // ------------------------
56:     always_comb begin
57:         // default signal assignment
58:         state_d    = state_q;
59:         address_n  = address_q;
60:         trans_id_n = trans_id_q;
61: 
62:         // we'll answer a write request only if we got address and data
63:         axi_resp_o.aw_ready = 1'b0;
64:         axi_resp_o.w_ready  = 1'b0;
65:         axi_resp_o.b_valid  = 1'b0;
66: 
67:         axi_resp_o.ar_ready = 1'b0;
68:         axi_resp_o.r_valid  = 1'b0;
69: 
70:         address_o      = '0;
71:         we_o           = 1'b0;
72:         en_o           = 1'b0;
73: 
74:         case (state_q)
75:             // we are ready to accept a new request
76:             IDLE: begin
77:                 // we've git a valid write request, we also know that we have asserted the aw_ready
78:                 if (axi_req_i.aw_valid) begin
79:                     axi_resp_o.aw_ready = 1'b1;
80:                     // this costs performance but the interconnect does not obey the AXI standard
81:                     // e.g.: we could wait for aw_valid && w_valid to do the transaction.
